title: Virtual Print Hierarchy
prompt_md:
## Goal
Complete the `print` overrides so `operator<<` dispatches correctly through the hierarchy.

## Input
No input.

## Output
Print exactly three lines:
```
Animal
Dog: Rex
Cat: Whiskers
```

## Requirements
1. `Animal` has `virtual void print(std::ostream& out) const` that writes `Animal`. It has a `friend operator<<` that calls `a.print(out)` and returns `out`.
2. `Dog` has `std::string name_` set by its constructor. Override `print` to write `Dog: ` followed by `name_`.
3. `Cat` has `std::string name_` set by its constructor. Override `print` to write `Cat: ` followed by `name_`.
4. `main` creates `Animal a`, `Dog d{"Rex"}`, `Cat c{"Whiskers"}` and stores them in `Animal* arr[3]`. Loop and print each with `std::cout << *arr[i]`.

title: Missing Virtual Print Bug
prompt_md:
## Goal
Fix the single bug so the program prints the correct derived-class name for each shape instead of always printing `Shape`.

## Input
No input.

## Output
Print exactly two lines:
```
Circle
Triangle
```

## Requirements
1. `Shape` has a virtual `print` member and a `friend operator<<` that calls it.
2. `Circle` and `Triangle` must each override `print` so dispatch prints the correct name.
3. The bug is in one of the derived class method signatures — find and fix it so virtual dispatch works.
4. Do not change `main` or `Shape`.
